title: Shri Khatu Shyam Ji Temple, Rajasthan
intro: The Khatu Shyam Ji Temple, located in the Sikar district of Rajasthan, is one of the most prominent pilgrimage sites in India.
description:
Shri Khatu Shyam Ji Temple – Khatu, Rajasthan
Introduction
The Khatu Shyam Ji Temple, located in the Sikar district of Rajasthan, is one of the most prominent pilgrimage sites in India. Dedicated to Khatu Shyam (Barbarik), the temple attracts millions of devotees who believe that the deity grants their wishes and removes their sufferings. It is a center of immense spiritual energy and devotion.
Temple Information
 Temple Name: Shri Khatu Shyam Ji Temple
 Location: Khatu Village, Sikar District, Rajasthan
 Presiding Deity: Khatu Shyam Ji (Barbarik)
 Significance: Known as the 'God of the Kali Yuga'.
History and Significance
Barbarik, the grandson of Bhima, possessed divine powers. Before the Mahabharata war, Lord Krishna asked for his head as a sacrifice. Impressed by his supreme sacrifice, Lord Krishna granted him the boon that in the Kali Yuga, he would be worshipped by the name "Shyam." The temple was built at the site where his head was discovered.
How to Reach
✈️ By Air:
 Jaipur International Airport: The nearest major airport (approx. 80–90 km). Taxis and buses are readily available from Jaipur to Khatu.
🚆 By Train:
 Ringas Junction (RGS): The nearest railway station (approx. 17 km). It is well-connected to major cities. Taxis and local transport are easily available from the station to the temple.
🛣️ By Road:
 Khatu is well-connected to Jaipur, Sikar, and Delhi via the national highway. Private and state transport buses are available.
Best Time to Visit
 October to March: The weather is comfortable and ideal for visiting.
 Phalgun Mela: Held annually (usually in February/March), this is the most auspicious time to visit if you wish to experience the grand festivities.
Temple Timings
🕒 Timing: Generally from 4:30 AM to 9:00 PM.
(Note: Timings can vary during major festivals or special events.)
Main Festivals
 Phalgun Mela: The biggest festival, attracting millions of devotees who often walk to the temple as a gesture of faith.
 Janmashtami: Celebrated with great enthusiasm.
Nearby Attractions
 Salasar Balaji Temple: Often visited by devotees as part of the same pilgrimage tour (located about 100 km away).
 Jeen Mata Temple: Another revered Shakti Peeth in the Sikar region.
Dress Code
 Modest and traditional attire is recommended to respect the sanctity of the temple.
Facilities for Devotees
 Organized queue system for Darshan.
 Various Dharamshalas and guest houses in Khatu village.
 Ample facilities for Prasad and offerings.
Conclusion
A visit to the Khatu Shyam Ji Temple is a deeply spiritual experience. The atmosphere, filled with chants of "Jai Shri Shyam," instills a sense of peace and hope in every devotee. It stands as a powerful symbol of faith, sacrifice, and divine grace.

faq.a1: According to religious beliefs, Khatu Shyam Ji is the brave Barbarik from the Mahabharata, to whom Lord Shri Krishna granted the boon of being worshipped as 'Shyam' in the Kali Yuga.
faq.q2: Where is the Khatu Shyam temple located?
faq.a2: It is located in Khatu village, Sikar district, Rajasthan.
faq.q3: What is the Khatu Shyam temple famous for?
faq.a3: It is famous for the devotion to Baba Shyam, the grand Phalgun Mela (fair), and the deep, unwavering faith of the devotees.
faq.q4: What is the best time to visit the Khatu Shyam temple?
faq.a4: The period from October to March is considered ideal for travel due to the pleasant weather.
faq.q5: What is the biggest festival of Khatu Shyam Ji?
faq.a5: The Phalgun Mela is considered the biggest and most famous festival.
